title: Media routes cleanup: over-fetching, duplication, organization
analysis:
media.py (353 lines) has 7 non-blocking review issues from task 44. After thorough analysis:

(S5) _parse_metadata_json: Identical try/except json.loads blocks at lines 131-136 (media_detail_page) and 181-186 (media_tab metadata branch). Extract to a shared helper.

(S4) _aggregate_detections: Same loop/counting pattern at lines 206-222 (media_tab detections branch) and 335-347 (api_media_detections). The tab version also builds frame_details; the API version does not. A shared helper can return all three (total, classes, frame_details) and callers pick what they need.

(S6+S12) Time formatters: _format_duration (line 16) omits hours if <1hr, None->'--:--'. _format_timestamp (line 151, after route handlers) always pads hours, None->'--:--:--'. They serve genuinely different purposes (display duration vs timeline position). Unify into a single _format_seconds(seconds, pad_hours=False) with the pad_hours flag controlling both zero-padding and the None fallback. Move _format_timestamp definition next to _format_duration at top of module.

(S1) Over-fetching: media_tab() calls get_media_detail() for every tab click, which runs 6 DB queries + face cluster join. Individual getter methods already exist in CatalogDB (get_media, get_transcript, get_detections_for_media, get_faces_for_media, get_audio_events_for_media, count_embeddings_for_media, get_face_clusters_by_ids). Refactor to dispatch per tab, calling only the methods needed. Face BLOB stripping (currently in get_media_detail) must be replicated in the faces tab branch.

(S8) API contract: /api/media/{id}/transcript returns 404 for missing transcript; /api/media/{id} returns null. Standardize: 404 only for missing parent media; missing analysis data returns 200 with empty payload. Change transcript endpoint to return {"language": null, "segments": []} when transcript is absent.

(S11) Redundant template context: detail.html and tab_metadata.html only reference 'media', 'extra_metadata', 'format_duration'. The 'detail' dict is passed but never used. Remove it.

Existing test suite (test_media_detail.py, 754 lines) covers all endpoints comprehensively including robustness tests for malformed JSON. Tests use file-backed CatalogDB + Starlette TestClient.

steps.description: Write failing unit tests for _parse_metadata_json(media_row) helper: returns parsed dict for valid JSON, returns {} for malformed JSON, returns {} for None/missing key. Import directly from media module.

Extract _parse_metadata_json(media) helper in media.py (after _format_duration). Takes a media dict, returns parsed metadata_json or {} on error. Replace duplicate blocks in media_detail_page (lines 131-136) and media_tab metadata branch (lines 181-186) with calls to the helper.

Write failing unit tests for _aggregate_detections(det_rows) helper: returns NamedTuple(total, classes, frame_details) for normal data; handles empty list; handles malformed detections_json gracefully; verifies frame_details has correct structure with number and count keys.

Extract _aggregate_detections(det_rows) helper in media.py returning a DetectionSummary NamedTuple(total, classes, frame_details). Replace duplicate logic in media_tab detections branch (lines 206-222) and api_media_detections (lines 335-347) with calls to the helper. api_media_detections ignores frame_details.

Write failing unit tests for unified _format_seconds(seconds, pad_hours=False) helper: pad_hours=False gives M:SS for <1hr and HH:MM:SS for >=1hr with None->'--:--'; pad_hours=True always gives 00:MM:SS with None->'--:--:--'. Parametrize covering 0s, 59s, 3600s, 3661s, None for both modes.

Replace _format_duration and _format_timestamp with a single _format_seconds(seconds, pad_hours=False) helper at top of media.py (line ~16). Create thin wrappers _format_duration=partial(_format_seconds, pad_hours=False) and _format_timestamp=partial(_format_seconds, pad_hours=True). Remove old _format_timestamp from line 151 (fixes S12 placement). All callers/templates unchanged.

Write failing tests for per-tab data fetching (S1): add TestTabPerTabFetching class that uses unittest.mock to patch individual CatalogDB methods, verifying that e.g. transcript tab calls get_transcript but NOT get_detections_for_media or get_faces_for_media. Cover all 6 tabs.

Refactor media_tab() to replace the single get_media_detail() call with per-tab DB method dispatch. Start with db.get_media(media_id) for existence check. Then: metadata needs nothing extra; transcript calls get_transcript; detections calls get_detections_for_media; faces calls get_faces_for_media + get_face_clusters_by_ids with BLOB stripping; audio_events calls get_audio_events_for_media; embeddings calls count_embeddings_for_media. All existing integration tests must still pass.

Update TestApiMediaTranscript.test_returns_404_when_media_has_no_transcript to expect 200 with {language: null, segments: []} instead of 404 (S8 contract fix). This test will now fail against current code.
Change api_media_transcript() to return {"language": null, "segments": []} with 200 when transcript is None, instead of raising HTTPException(404). Keep 404 only for missing parent media.

Write explicit test asserting the detail page template context does not include 'detail' key — verify existing test_returns_200_html and test_metadata_tab_rendered_by_default still pass, confirming the template never needed it.
Remove 'detail' key from the template context dict in media_detail_page() (line 144). Only pass 'media', 'extra_metadata', 'format_duration'. Run full test suite to confirm no regression.

design_decisions.decision: Use functools.partial for _format_duration/_format_timestamp wrappers instead of lambdas
design_decisions.rationale: partial preserves the function signature for introspection and has a readable repr. Keeps template context passing unchanged — templates still receive format_duration and format_timestamp by name.

design_decisions.decision: Return NamedTuple from _aggregate_detections rather than a plain tuple
design_decisions.rationale: Named fields (total, classes, frame_details) are self-documenting at call sites. NamedTuple is lighter than dataclass and naturally unpacks.

design_decisions.decision: Standardize API contract: 404 only for missing parent media, 200 with empty payload for missing analysis data
design_decisions.rationale: Clients shouldn't need to distinguish between 'media has no transcript yet' and 'transcript data is empty'. This aligns with the aggregate /api/media/{id} endpoint which returns null for transcript. Makes API consumption uniform across endpoints.

design_decisions.decision: Keep face BLOB stripping in the media_tab faces branch rather than adding a db.py helper
design_decisions.rationale: BLOB stripping is a presentation concern. The DB layer (get_faces_for_media) correctly returns raw rows. get_media_detail already handles this for the aggregate case. The tab handler strips BLOBs locally, matching the existing pattern. No db.py changes needed.

design_decisions.decision: Use mock-based tests for verifying per-tab fetching (S1) rather than only relying on integration tests
design_decisions.rationale: Integration tests verify correctness but can't verify the absence of over-fetching. Mock-based tests patch specific CatalogDB methods to assert only required methods are called per tab, ensuring the performance improvement is actually enforced.

design_decisions.decision: No modifications to db.py — all changes confined to media.py and tests
design_decisions.rationale: CatalogDB already has all needed granular methods (get_media, get_transcript, get_detections_for_media, etc.). The over-fetching problem is in the route layer calling the wrong aggregator method. No new DB methods are needed.

reuse.where: autopilot/db.py: get_media(), get_transcript(), get_detections_for_media(), get_faces_for_media(), get_audio_events_for_media(), count_embeddings_for_media(), get_face_clusters_by_ids()
reuse.how: Use these existing per-table methods in media_tab() instead of get_media_detail() to fetch only the data needed for each tab.

reuse.what: Existing test fixtures and seeded DB
reuse.how: Reuse these fixtures for new helper unit tests and per-tab fetching tests. The seeded DB has test1 (full data) and test2 (no data) covering both happy and empty paths.

reuse.what: Existing integration test suite as regression safety net
reuse.how: These 15+ integration tests verify correctness of all endpoints. All must continue passing after the S1 refactor and other changes, serving as a comprehensive regression gate.
Use partial(_format_seconds, pad_hours=False/True) to create _format_duration/_format_timestamp wrappers that preserve the existing caller interface without code duplication.
